results.possibleIdentity: John B. Lyons Jr. was an electronics and microwave engineer active in New York during the late 1960s and 1970s. According to historical records from the Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), a non-profit organization that facilitated collaborations between artists and engineers, he was registered as a technical expert available for such partnerships. At the time of his association with E.A.T., he was employed by the International Telephone & Telegraph Corporation (IT&T) and specialized in the technical fields of electronics and microwaves. He is likely the same John Lyons (1948–2019) who became a prominent figure in the New York radio frequency (RF) industry, serving as the Assistant Vice President and Director of Broadcast Communications for The Durst Organization. This John Lyons was known for leading the design and implementation of master antennas at Four Times Square and the broadcast transmission facility at One World Trade Center, and had earlier held engineering positions with various New York-based broadcast organizations involving point-to-point microwave and satellite communications systems.
